deep_dive.title: Individual Control Barrier Functions-Guided Diffusion Model for Safe Offline Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Offline reinforcement learning allows control policies to be learned directly from data without online interaction, making it suitable for safety-critical tasks. Recent studies have applied diffusion models to offline reinforcement learning to leverage their strong capacity for modeling complex data distributions. However, existing approaches primarily focus on single-agent settings, leaving the safety challenges in multi-agent environments largely unexplored. In this work, we propose a safe offline multi-agent reinforcement learning algorithm that embeds neural individual control barrier functions into the diffusion model to enhance safety during trajectory generation, with control policies recovered through inverse dynamics. We evaluate our algorithm across diverse benchmarks, demonstrating substantial safety improvements while maintaining competitive rewards.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: neural individual CBF embedded in diffusion model for safe offline multi-agent RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 将神经个体控制屏障函数嵌入扩散模型实现安全离线多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 将神经个体控制屏障函数嵌入扩散模型实现安全离线多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 将神经个体控制屏障函数嵌入扩散模型实现安全离线多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes embedding neural individual control barrier functions into diffusion models for safe offline multi-agent RL, achieving safety during trajectory generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有离线多智能体强化学习在安全挑战方面研究不足。本文提出一种安全离线MARL算法，将神经个体控制屏障函数嵌入扩散模型，在轨迹生成中增强安全性，并通过隐式策略恢复。实验表明该方法在多智能体环境中有效保证安全，同时保持任务性能。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: safe reinforcement learning control barrier function

deep_dive.title: Embedding ISO 10218 Safety Compliance in Robots via Control Barrier Functions for Human-Robot Collaboration
deep_dive.abstract: Human-Robot Collaboration (HRC) requires strict adherence to safety standards, such as ISO 10218, to prevent harmful interactions. Standard Speed and Separation Monitoring (SSM) filters calculate safe robotic speeds based on conservative assumptions, such as constant human velocity, which prevents accurate predictions of minimum separation distances and causes unnecessary operational halts. This paper proposes a Control Barrier Function (CBF) that explicitly incorporates human acceleration data to analytically forward-predict the minimum human-robot separation distance during a worst-case robotic stopping trajectory. To guarantee safety at the control level, this predictive CBF is integrated as an inequality constraint within a Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP) framework. Specifically, two methods are proposed: Method I, a CBF-constrained PD safety filter; and Method II, a task-scaling SQP controller that enforces a spatial tube constraint. Simulated and real-world experiments on a UR10e robot evaluate the two proposed methods against a standard industrial SSM module baseline. Results demonstrate that Method II dynamically modulates execution speed and confines spatial deviations. Compared to Method I, Method II achieves a 63\% reduction in mean trajectory error and avoids excessive evasive manoeuvres, ensuring high task throughput while complying with ISO 10218 SSM guidelines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: CBF for human-robot collaboration safety compliance with ISO 10218
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用控制屏障函数实现人机协作安全合规，符合ISO 10218
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用控制屏障函数实现人机协作安全合规，符合ISO 10218
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用控制屏障函数实现人机协作安全合规，符合ISO 10218
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a predictive Control Barrier Function incorporating human acceleration for human-robot collaboration, guaranteeing safety at control level.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 人机协作需要严格安全标准。本文提出一种控制屏障函数，显式融入人体加速度数据，向前预测最小人机分离距离，并作为不等式约束集成到控制层保证安全。实验表明该方法减少保守停机，提高协作效率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 人机协作需要严格安全标准。本文提出一种控制屏障函数，显式融入人体加速度数据，向前预测最小人机分离距离，并作为不等式约束集成到控制层保证安全。实验表明该方法减少保守停机，提高协作效率。

quick_skim.title: Patching Control Lyapunov Barrier Functions for Temporal Logic Specifications with Bounded Controls
quick_skim.abstract: We propose an abstraction-free framework for controller synthesis for continuous-time dynamical systems subject to Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) specifications and bounded control inputs. The proposed method combines the sequential decomposition of LTL tasks with the use of formally certified Control Lyapunov-Barrier Functions (CLBFs). By formulating local specifications as a sequence of safe-stabilization problems, we systematically approximate and patch the winning sets of the decomposed subtasks. The satisfaction of these local constraints is guaranteed by the offline-computed level sets of the CLBFs. As a result, our framework yields formally verified switching feedback controllers that enable efficient online planning and dynamic re-planning. This ensures robust continuous specification satisfaction in the presence of state perturbations, avoiding the explicit state-space abstractions commonly required in the literature. The approach is validated through numerical simulations and a hardware demonstration on a Crazyflie quadrotor.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses Control Lyapunov-Barrier Functions for safe control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用控制李雅普诺夫屏障函数实现安全控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用控制李雅普诺夫屏障函数实现安全控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用控制李雅普诺夫屏障函数实现安全控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an abstraction-free controller synthesis framework using patched CLBFs to satisfy LTL specifications with bounded controls, enabling formally verified switching feedback controllers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一种无抽象框架，通过修补控制李雅普诺夫屏障函数（CLBF）来满足有界控制下的线性时序逻辑规范，获得形式化验证的切换反馈控制器，实现高效在线规划与安全稳定控制。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出一种无抽象框架，通过修补控制李雅普诺夫屏障函数（CLBF）来满足有界控制下的线性时序逻辑规范，获得形式化验证的切换反馈控制器，实现高效在线规划与安全稳定控制。
